nodes.event: User requests a work overview for the past two weeks (GitHub commits + emails + meetings)
Deep dive into GitHub activity: issue statuses, PR merge status
Email content correlation: blocker identification and resolution
Slack discussion analysis: technical decision (WebSocket vs SSE)
Notion progress verification and cross-platform cross-verification
Comprehensive risk analysis and recommendations (all-platform reasoning)

rounds.user_message: Help me review my work over the past 3 weeks. How many code commits did I make on GitHub? How many emails related to the SmartHome project did I receive? How many meetings did I attend?

questions.question: Regarding the work data from the past 3 weeks, which set of statistics is correct? (GitHub commit count / SmartHome-related email count / total meetings)
questions.explanation: GitHub commits #6-#12 total 7; SmartHome-related emails E1-E6 total 6 (excluding E7 AWS billing and E8 Rust sharing); meetings C1-C9 total 9
questions.source: GitHub commits + Gmail E1-E8 + Calendar C1-C9

rounds.user_message: Tell me more about the GitHub SmartHome activity. Which issues have been closed? Are there any unmerged PRs?

questions.question: Regarding the issue and PR statuses in the GitHub SmartHome repository, which description is entirely correct?

options.A: Closed issues: #3 and #5; Open issues: #4, #6, #7; PR #8 and #9 are both merged; no unmerged PRs
options.B: Closed issues: #3 and #4; Open issues: #5, #6, #7; PR #8 is merged, #9 is unmerged
options.C: Closed issues: #3, #5, and #6; Open issues: #4 and #7; PR #8 and #9 are both merged
options.D: Closed issues: #3 and #5; Open issues: #4, #6, #7; PR #8 is merged, #9 is pending review

questions.explanation: Closed: #3 implement temperature sensor data display, #5 fix mobile layout misalignment; Open: #4 user authentication module, #6 add device offline alert, #7 performance optimization; PR #8 and #9 are both merged, no unmerged PRs

rounds.user_message: In those emails you just mentioned, were there any blockers or issues mentioned?

questions.question: According to the email records, what are the blockers mentioned in the project and their resolution status?

options.A: The blocker is MQTT broker deployment delay (E1 weekly report), later confirmed to be Docker network configuration error and port mapping conflict, resolved (E5)
options.B: The blocker is WebSocket connection instability (E1 weekly report), later confirmed to be a server configuration issue, resolved (E5)
options.C: The blocker is MQTT broker deployment delay (E3 review), still unresolved
options.D: The blocker is database connection timeout (E1 weekly report), later confirmed to be connection pool configuration error, resolved (E5)

questions.explanation: E1 weekly report mentioned MQTT broker deployment delay as a blocker, E5 confirmed it was resolved, the cause was Docker network configuration error and port mapping conflict

rounds.user_message: What was the most discussed topic on Slack? Were any technical decisions made on Slack?

questions.question: What was the key technical decision made on Slack? What was the final conclusion and the reason?

options.A: WebSocket vs SSE discussion, ultimately chose WebSocket because SSE is unstable in certain proxy environments
options.B: WebSocket vs SSE discussion, ultimately chose SSE because WebSocket has higher implementation complexity
options.C: MQTT vs RabbitMQ discussion, ultimately chose MQTT because it is more suitable for IoT scenarios
options.D: REST vs GraphQL discussion, ultimately chose REST because the team is more familiar with it

questions.explanation: S7 raised the WebSocket vs SSE discussion, S8 made the final decision to use WebSocket because SSE is unstable in certain proxy environments

rounds.user_message: How was the MQTT issue discussed and resolved across different platforms? Help me piece together the timeline.

questions.question: Regarding the cross-platform timeline of the MQTT issue, which sequence and content is correct?

options.A: Slack(S3) raised deployment issue -> Gmail(E1) weekly report listed as blocker -> Gmail(E5) confirmed Docker port mapping conflict resolved -> Slack(S4) confirmed resolution
options.B: Gmail(E1) weekly report raised issue -> Slack(S3) team discussion -> GitHub submitted fix PR -> Slack(S4) confirmed resolution
options.C: GitHub created issue -> Slack(S3) discussion -> Gmail(E5) confirmed network configuration issue -> Slack(S4) confirmed resolution
options.D: Slack(S3) raised deployment issue -> Gmail(E1) weekly report listed as blocker -> GitHub submitted hotfix -> Gmail(E5) confirmed resolution

questions.explanation: Slack S3 raised the MQTT broker deployment issue, E1 weekly report mentioned it as a blocker, E5 confirmed Docker network configuration error/port mapping conflict was resolved, S4 confirmed resolution. GitHub temperature sensor issue #3/PR #8 is an indirect correlation

rounds.user_message: What's the current project progress according to the technical design document on Notion? Is it consistent with the information you collected from other platforms?

questions.question: Regarding the project milestone statuses recorded in Notion and their cross-verification results with other platforms, which is correct?

options.A: M1 Basic Framework completed; M2 Sensor Data completed; M3 User Authentication in progress; M4 Alert System planned; M5 Performance Optimization planned; M2 can be verified through GitHub PR #8 merged, M3 can be verified through Sprint 4 planning email (E6)
options.B: M1 Basic Framework completed; M2 Sensor Data in progress; M3 User Authentication planned; M4 Alert System planned; M5 Performance Optimization planned; M2 is still in progress, GitHub PR #8 pending review
options.C: M1 Basic Framework completed; M2 Sensor Data completed; M3 User Authentication completed; M4 Alert System in progress; M5 Performance Optimization planned; all completed items can be verified through GitHub
options.D: M1 Basic Framework completed; M2 Sensor Data completed; M3 User Authentication in progress; M4 Alert System planned; M5 Performance Optimization planned; but M2 is inconsistent with GitHub records, PR #8 not merged

questions.explanation: Notion N1 records 5 milestone statuses correctly; M2 is confirmed completed through GitHub PR #8 merged and Sprint retrospective; M3 is confirmed in progress through E6 Sprint 4 planning email
questions.source: Notion N1 + GitHub PR #8 + Gmail E6

rounds.user_message: What's my schedule for the past 3 weeks? Are there any conflicts with items mentioned in the emails?

questions.question: Regarding next week's schedule and its correlation with emails, which description is correct?

options.A: C8 Client Demo (2/17) + C9 Rust Tech Sharing (2/19); the Rust sharing mentioned in E8 email corresponds to C9, no time conflict
options.B: C8 Client Demo (2/17) + C9 Rust Tech Sharing (2/18); the Rust sharing mentioned in E8 email corresponds to C9, conflicts with client demo time
options.C: C8 Client Demo (2/17) + C9 Sprint Planning (2/19); the Sprint 4 mentioned in E6 email corresponds to C9, no time conflict
options.D: C8 Code Review (2/17) + C9 Rust Tech Sharing (2/19); the Rust sharing mentioned in E8 email corresponds to C9, no time conflict

questions.explanation: C8 Client Demo 2/17 10:00-11:30, C9 Rust Tech Sharing 2/19 14:00-15:00; E8 email mentions Rust tech sharing (next Wednesday 14:00) corresponding to C9, they are not on the same day so no conflict

rounds.user_message: Based on all the information you've collected, what do you think is the biggest risk for this project right now? What should we prioritize next?

questions.question: Based on all platform information combined, which risk analysis and priority recommendation is most reasonable?

options.A: Biggest risk: M5 Performance Optimization has not started and M3 User Authentication is still in progress, while client requirements (multilingual, data export, mobile adaptation) are not yet scheduled; should prioritize completing M3 and evaluating client requirement priorities
options.B: Biggest risk: MQTT deployment issue may recur; should prioritize strengthening MQTT module stability testing
options.C: Biggest risk: team is understaffed, 4 people cannot complete all milestones; should prioritize recruiting new members
options.D: Biggest risk: technology stack choice (WebSocket) may not be suitable for production; should prioritize technical validation

questions.explanation: M5 Performance Optimization has not started, M3 User Authentication is in progress but Sprint 4 has many goals, client requirements (multilingual, data export, mobile adaptation) are not yet scheduled. MQTT issue is already resolved, WebSocket has been confirmed through team discussion
questions.source: Notion N1 + Gmail E4/E6 + GitHub issues + Slack S7/S8
